sourceMovie.title: Yi Yi
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Yi Yi (2000) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Edward Yang's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Karate Kid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yi Yi clicked for you, The Karate Kid (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Graduate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yi Yi clicked for you, The Graduate (1967) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Yi Yi?
faq.answer: Try The Karate Kid, The Graduate, The Assassination of Jesse James by the Coward Robert Ford—each shares DNA with Yi Yi while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Yi Yi?
faq.answer: Yi Yi (2000) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Yi Yi worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
